A.name: Cecilia I. Márquez
A.role: Vice President, presiding in absence of President Weekly

llmResponse.agendaChanges: Item 13.10 (Acceptance of the Redwood City Teachers Association's Sunshine Proposal) was moved to immediately after Section 9 (Public Comment / Labor Association Comments), before the LCAP presentation (Section 10). All other items proceeded in original order.

items.title: Call to Order
Roll Call (Open Session / Reconvene)
Reconvene to Open Session / Roll Call / Report Out on Closed Session
Welcome / Pledge of Allegiance
Changes to the Agenda
Approval of the Agenda
Public Comment

publicComments.name: Brianna Gear
publicComments.summary: Spoke as a MIT staff member urging the board to fund the continuation of Nelson Brazil's student services coordinator position, describing him as essential to school safety, PBIS, and student well-being.

publicComments.name: Carolina Rodriguez
publicComments.summary: A science teacher at MIT Middle shared two student stories illustrating how Nelson Brazil's deep family relationships and restorative practices improved attendance, safety, and student outcomes, asking the board to preserve his position.

publicComments.name: Molly Cross
publicComments.summary: Read a letter from teacher Henri Kuhn urging the board to reverse the decision to eliminate the student services position, citing Nelson Brazil's contributions to school culture, collaboration, and continuity.

items.title: Labor Association Comments

publicComments.name: Amy Barstadt
publicComments.summary: RCTA negotiations chair presented the union's sunshine proposal, emphasizing good-faith bargaining and priorities of manageable class sizes and fair, competitive teacher compensation.

publicComments.name: Maria Stockton
publicComments.summary: CSEA president congratulated Trustee Li on his new child, invited board members to connect with CSEA, and reminded principals about the CSEA scholarship for eighth-grade students.

items.title: Acceptance of the Redwood City Teachers Association's Sunshine Proposal (moved earlier on agenda)
Presentation on the Local Control Accountability Plan (LCAP) Mid Year Report

publicComments.name: Wendy Brown
publicComments.summary: MTSS TOSA at Roosevelt described the district-wide TOSA attendance support efforts, including student engagement plans, SARB meetings, home visits, and identified barriers such as transportation and school avoidance, particularly in TK/Kinder.

items.title: Review of Draft Resolution: Calling An Election, Establishing Specifications Of The Election Order, And Requesting Consolidation With Other Elections Occurring On June 2, 2026

publicComments.name: David Camarena
publicComments.summary: Principal at Taft expressed strong support for the parcel tax, describing how it would fund STEAM, counseling, professional development, and staffing stability at his school.

publicComments.name: Carl Landers
publicComments.summary: Supported the parcel tax but urged the board to publicly share their vision for the district's half of the funds (especially teacher salary increases) and questioned whether the 17.5-cent rate was high enough given no higher rate was polled.

publicComments.name: Ying Chen
publicComments.summary: Orion principal and SRAC representative described how budget cuts have reduced critical positions at her school and argued the parcel tax is essential to maintaining reading intervention, MTSS, Mandarin coaching, and guest teacher coverage.

publicComments.name: Rupert
publicComments.summary: Hoover Community School principal advocated for the parcel tax as a way to protect staffing, reading and math interventions, English learner supports, mental health services, and enrichment opportunities for high-need students.

items.title: Approval of Consent Items
Submission of 2026 California School Boards Association (CSBA) Delegate Assembly Nominations
Adoption of Resolution No. 17, Reduction or Elimination of Certificated Services for Fiscal Year 2025-2026
Adoption of Resolution No. 18, Release and Reassignment of Certificated Administrator(s)
Adoption of Resolution No. 19, Determination of Order of Employment for Certificated Employees for Elimination of or Reductions in Particular Kinds of Services for Fiscal Year 2025-2026
Adoption of Resolution No. 20, Reduction of Certain Classified Services for Fiscal Year 2025-2026
Approval of Non-Reelection of Certificated Probationary Personnel
Approval of Release of Temporary Teachers
Approval of Personnel Changes for the 2025-2026 School Year
Receipt of Governing Board's Initial Proposal for Negotiations with Redwood City Teachers Association for the 2026-2027 School Year
Board and Superintendent Reports
Other Business / Suggested Items for Future Agenda
Board of Trustees Meeting Reflection
Board of Trustees Meeting Calendar
Adjournment
